title: Insert multiple rows
summary: Learn how to insert multiple rows into a table in a single SQL statement using the inventory_ops dataset.

sketchBlocks.title: Why insert multiple rows at once?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Inserting multiple rows in a single SQL statement is more efficient than inserting them one at a time. It reduces the number of commands sent to the database and can help keep related data changes together. For example, to add two new items to the `inventory_items` table:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10'),
  ('Coffee Thermos', 'Kitchen', 18.00, 'draft', 0, 'New product', '2026-02-10');
```
This statement adds both rows at once.

sketchBlocks.title: Syntax for inserting multiple rows
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for inserting multiple rows is:

```sql
INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2, ...)
VALUES
  (value1a, value2a, ...),
  (value1b, value2b, ...),
  ... ;
```
Each set of parentheses represents a new row. All rows must have values for the same columns, in the same order.

sketchBlocks.title: Using explicit column lists
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
It's best practice to specify the columns you are inserting into. This makes your SQL more robust, especially if the table structure changes later. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 12.00, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-11'),
  ('Lunch Box', 'Kitchen', 8.50, 'draft', 0, NULL, '2026-02-11');
```
This ensures each value matches the correct column.

quizDraft.title: Insert two new inventory items in one statement
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert the following two items into the `inventory_items` table in a single SQL statement. Use the columns: name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated.

- 'Travel Mug', 'Kitchen', 13.00, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-12'
- 'Sticker Sheet', 'Accessories', 2.50, 'draft', 0, 'Limited edition', '2026-02-12'

Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Use INSERT INTO with a column list and two sets of values.

help.concept: To insert multiple rows, use one INSERT statement with multiple value sets, each in parentheses and separated by commas.
help.hint_1: Start with INSERT INTO, list the columns, then VALUES, and provide both rows in parentheses.
help.hint_2: Make sure each row has the same columns and the values are in the correct order.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Travel Mug', 'Kitchen', 13.00, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-12'),
  ('Sticker Sheet', 'Accessories', 2.50, 'draft', 0, 'Limited edition', '2026-02-12');

quizDraft.title: Insert rows with NULL and default values
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert two new rows into `inventory_items` with the following data. Use NULL for notes and let status default for both rows. Use columns: name, category, price, is_test, notes, last_updated.

- 'Sample Pen', 'Accessories', 1.25, 1, NULL, '2026-02-13'
- 'Test Apron', 'Kitchen', 11.00, 1, NULL, '2026-02-13'

Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Omit the status column to use its default value.

help.concept: If you omit a column in the column list, the database uses its default value for that column.
help.hint_1: List only the columns you want to set. The rest will use defaults or NULL if allowed.
help.hint_2: Make sure your VALUES match the columns you listed, and status will default to 'draft'.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Sample Pen', 'Accessories', 1.25, 1, NULL, '2026-02-13'),
  ('Test Apron', 'Kitchen', 11.00, 1, NULL, '2026-02-13');

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit columns and values
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert two new items into `inventory_items` with these details. Use only the columns: name, category, price, status, last_updated. Omit is_test and notes (let them default or be NULL).

- 'Gift Card', 'Accessories', 20.00, 'active', '2026-02-14'
- 'Sample Plate', 'Kitchen', 6.00, 'draft', '2026-02-14'

Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: List only the required columns and match the values to them.

help.concept: You can insert rows by specifying only the columns you want to set; omitted columns use their default values or NULL.
help.hint_1: Write the column list for just the five columns you need.
help.hint_2: Each VALUES row should have five values, matching your column list.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Gift Card', 'Accessories', 20.00, 'active', '2026-02-14'),
  ('Sample Plate', 'Kitchen', 6.00, 'draft', '2026-02-14');

quizDraft.title: Benefits of inserting multiple rows at once
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are benefits of inserting multiple rows in a single SQL statement? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about efficiency and data consistency.

help.concept: Inserting multiple rows at once can improve performance and help keep related data changes together.
help.hint_1: Consider how fewer statements affect speed and transaction safety.
help.hint_2: Look for options about speed, fewer commands, and keeping data together.

options.content: Reduces the number of database commands sent
Helps keep related data changes together

quizDraft.title: Syntax for multiple row insert
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: To insert multiple rows in one statement, you separate each set of values with a ____.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how you separate items in a list.

help.concept: In SQL, each set of values for a new row is separated by a comma in the VALUES clause.
help.hint_1: What punctuation separates each row's values in the VALUES clause?
help.hint_2: It's the same symbol used to separate items in a list.

quizDraft.template: INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Item1', 'Category1', 1.00) ____ ('Item2', 'Category2', 2.00);
